text:
There were three mice in a garden. They ate everything in the garden, leaving no seeds for the next year's plants. The sun was setting, casting long shadows all around. One mouse, named Mousey, felt a pang of guilt. He remembered seeing a single, fallen fruit from a tree. That had been the only piece of something good left in the garden.

Mousey decided to go back to that tree. The tree had seen much less action than the rest of the garden and was full of fruit. After a careful nibble, Mousey began to eat slowly. As the day wore on, he felt himself savoring the taste instead of devouring it. In the middle of the day, while the other two mice were busy fighting over the last piece, Mousey realized that he had found solace and a purpose beyond the chaos of their gluttony.

The next day, Mousey returned to that tree, and the other two mice noticed a changed aura about him
